$comment: Cluster-F F3 (float_mul_sub): float `*` and `-` on double and float, end-to-end. Witnesses the encodings this cycle added — x86 MULSD/MULSS (0F 59) + SUBSD/SUBSS (0F 5C), arm64 FMUL (0x1E600800) + FSUB (0x1E603800). Each op is in a non-inlined function so operands are runtime values (not const-folded), and the `release` arm runs the shipped pipeline. SUBTRACTION is NON-COMMUTATIVE: a-b vs b-a pinned distinct (returns 3/6) so a reversed operand wire is caught. exit 42 == all ops + order correct.
